textPlain: > In the Swiftie-verse, an “Easter egg” refers to a hidden clue or detail that Taylor plants in her work to suggest something big (like a new album) is comingI refuse to believe that the authors actually think Taylor Swift invented the "Easter Egg", is this the engineering version of discussion-bait?Besides, not much of an easter egg if you give it away in the very next paragraph, that name kinda implies how you're supposed to do it.
This is a very interesting way to get someone to notice both your product and the flexibility of your product. Not your typical ad/demo. Kudos to Grafana.
So this is what they do when they aren’t reinventing their metrics database engine for the fifth time?

> I refuse to believe that the authors actually think Taylor Swift invented the "Easter Egg"In the words of the first ever female action star, Jennifer Lawrence, "People will believe anything if they know nothing."

I think they we're just defining what Easter egg meant in this context.The fact that it has an "Easter egg," in this context, is more of a more meta nod, rather than it being a hidden thing you have to find, since that is a large part of the culture.And who knows, maybe there are others that they didn't show!
